methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainbow Robotics, a South Korean robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oh Jun-ho, was acquired after raising $50M+ in capital. The target operates within a sector represented by a limited public comparable set (1 listed peer, ticker 277810.KQ) and a sparse private market, with 8 identified private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable to this target. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers in total, but none met the minimum threshold of three comparable companies when filtered by stage, investment round proximity, or valuation tier. The single public comparable and fragmented private peer set lack sufficient homogeneity to support reliable median multiple derivation. A broad sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given heterogeneity in business model maturity, geographic exposure, and technology focus across the peer universe.

Given the acquisition status and data constraints, traditional triangulation methods cannot generate a defensible valuation range. Any estimate would require either (1) acceptance of wide confidence intervals using all-sector comparables at high risk of bias, or (2) transaction-specific analysis incorporating deal structure, buyer strategic rationale, and earnout provisions—none of which are present in the available dataset. The valuation exercise is constrained by private market illiquidity and insufficient narrow-fit peers rather than by quantifiable company fundamentals.
